req.title: Inter-facility referral (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility; the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
pytest test suite for:
  Requirement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility;
               the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check.
  Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
"""

import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (production code placeholders – replace with real imports)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class Referral:
    referral_id: str
    clinician_id: str
    patient_id: str
    referring_facility_id: str
    receiving_facility_id: str
    priority: int                        # 1 (routine) – 5 (emergency)
    created_at: datetime
    expires_at: datetime
    notes: str = ""
    consent_obtained: bool = False


@dataclass
class SecureMessage:
    message_id: str
    referral_id: str
    sender_id: str
    recipient_facility_id: str
    sent_at: datetime
    encrypted: bool = True
    payload: dict = field(default_factory=dict)


@dataclass
class ConsentRecord:
    consent_id: str
    patient_id: str
    referral_id: str
    consented: bool
    checked_at: datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service interfaces (production code placeholders)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ReferralService:
    def __init__(self, message_gateway, consent_service):
        self._msg = message_gateway
        self._consent = consent_service

    def send_referral(self, referral: Referral) -> dict:
        """
        Validates and sends a referral.
        Returns a result dict with keys: success, message_id, consent_id.
        Raises ValueError for invalid input.
        """
        # --- validation (minimal stub; real impl lives in production code) ---
        if referral.referring_facility_id == referral.receiving_facility_id:
            raise ValueError("Referring and receiving facilities must differ.")
        if not (1 <= referral.priority <= 5):
            raise ValueError(f"Priority {referral.priority} out of range [1,5].")
        if referral.expires_at <= referral.created_at:
            raise ValueError("expires_at must be after created_at.")

        # --- consent check ---
        consent = self._consent.check(referral)

        # --- secure message ---
        message = self._msg.send(referral)

        return {
            "success": True,
            "message_id": message.message_id,
            "consent_id": consent.consent_id,
        }


class MessageGateway:
    def send(self, referral: Referral) -> SecureMessage:
        raise NotImplementedError

class ConsentService:
    def check(self, referral: Referral) -> ConsentRecord:
        raise NotImplementedError


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def mock_message_gateway():
    gw = MagicMock(spec=MessageGateway)
    gw.send.return_value = SecureMessage(
        message_id="msg-001",
        referral_id="ref-001",
        sender_id="clinician-1",
        recipient_facility_id="facility-B",
        sent_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
        encrypted=True,
    )
    return gw


@pytest.fixture
def mock_consent_service():
    cs = MagicMock(spec=ConsentService)
    cs.check.return_value = ConsentRecord(
        consent_id="consent-001",
        patient_id="patient-1",
        referral_id="ref-001",
        consented=True,
        checked_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
    )
    return cs


@pytest.fixture
def referral_service(mock_message_gateway, mock_consent_service):
    return ReferralService(mock_message_gateway, mock_consent_service)


@pytest.fixture
def valid_referral():
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    return Referral(
        referral_id="ref-001",
        clinician_id="clinician-1",
        patient_id="patient-1",
        referring_facility_id="facility-A",
        receiving_facility_id="facility-B",
        priority=3,
        created_at=now,
        expires_at=now + timedelta(days=7),
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper factory
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def make_referral(**overrides) -> Referral:
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    defaults = dict(
        referral_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        clinician_id="clinician-1",
        patient_id="patient-1",
        referring_facility_id="facility-A",
        receiving_facility_id="facility-B",
        priority=3,
        created_at=now,
        expires_at=now + timedelta(days=7),
    )
    defaults.update(overrides)
    return Referral(**defaults)


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 1 – Happy-path / core requirement
# ===========================================================================

class TestSendReferralHappyPath:

    def test_send_referral_returns_success(self, referral_service, valid_referral):
        result = referral_service.send_referral(valid_referral)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_send_referral_returns_message_id(self, referral_service, valid_referral):
        result = referral_service.send_referral(valid_referral)
        assert "message_id" in result
        assert result["message_id"]  # non-empty

    def test_send_referral_returns_consent_id(self, referral_service, valid_referral):
        result = referral_service.send_referral(valid_referral)
        assert "consent_id" in result
        assert result["consent_id"]  # non-empty

    def test_secure_message_is_triggered_exactly_once(
        self, referral_service, valid_referral, mock_message_gateway
    ):
        referral_service.send_referral(valid_referral)
        mock_message_gateway.send.assert_called_once_with(valid_referral)

    def test_consent_check_is_triggered_exactly_once(
        self, referral_service, valid_referral, mock_consent_service
    ):
        referral_service.send_referral(valid_referral)
        mock_consent_service.check.assert_called_once_with(valid_referral)

    def test_message_is_encrypted(
        self, referral_service, valid_referral, mock_message_gateway
    ):
        referral_service.send_referral(valid_referral)
        sent_message: SecureMessage = mock_message_gateway.send.return_value
        assert sent_message.encrypted is True

    def test_consent_check_precedes_message_dispatch(
        self, referral_service, valid_referral, mock_message_gateway, mock_consent_service
    ):
        """Consent must be verified before the secure message is sent."""
        call_order = []
        mock_consent_service.check.side_effect = (
            lambda r: (call_order.append("consent"), mock_consent_service.check.return_value)[1]
        )
        mock_message_gateway.send.side_effect = (
            lambda r: (call_order.append("message"), mock_message_gateway.send.return_value)[1]
        )
        referral_service.send_referral(valid_referral)
        assert call_order.index("consent") < call_order.index("message")

    def test_referral_id_propagated_to_message(
        self, referral_service, valid_referral, mock_message_gateway
    ):
        referral_service.send_referral(valid_referral)
        args, _ = mock_message_gateway.send.call_args
        assert args[0].referral_id == valid_referral.referral_id

    def test_referral_id_propagated_to_consent(
        self, referral_service, valid_referral, mock_consent_service
    ):
        referral_service.send_referral(valid_referral)
        args, _ = mock_consent_service.check.call_args
        assert args[0].referral_id == valid_referral.referral_id


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 2 – Priority boundary cases (numeric element)
# ===========================================================================

class TestPriorityBoundary:
    """Priority is an integer in the closed interval [1, 5]."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("priority", [1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
    def test_valid_priority_accepted(self, referral_service, priority):
        ref = make_referral(priority=priority)
        result = referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_priority_minimum_boundary(self, referral_service):
        ref = make_referral(priority=1)
        result = referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_priority_maximum_boundary(self, referral_service):
        ref = make_referral(priority=5)
        result = referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_priority_below_minimum_raises(self, referral_service):
        ref = make_referral(priority=0)
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Priority"):
            referral_service.send_referral(ref)

    def test_priority_above_maximum_raises(self, referral_service):
        ref = make_referral(priority=6)
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Priority"):
            referral_service.send_referral(ref)

    def test_priority_negative_raises(self, referral_service):
        ref = make_referral(priority=-1)
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            referral_service.send_referral(ref)

    def test_priority_zero_raises(self, referral_service):
        ref = make_referral(priority=0)
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            referral_service.send_referral(ref)

    def test_priority_large_positive_raises(self, referral_service):
        ref = make_referral(priority=999)
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            referral_service.send_referral(ref)

    def test_priority_minimum_minus_one_raises(self, referral_service):
        """Off-by-one below lower bound."""
        ref = make_referral(priority=0)          # 1 - 1
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            referral_service.send_referral(ref)

    def test_priority_maximum_plus_one_raises(self, referral_service):
        """Off-by-one above upper bound."""
        ref = make_referral(priority=6)          # 5 + 1
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            referral_service.send_referral(ref)


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 3 – Timestamp boundary cases
# ===========================================================================

class TestTimestampBoundary:
    """expires_at must be strictly after created_at."""

    def test_expires_at_one_second_after_created_at(self, referral_service):
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        ref = make_referral(
            created_at=now,
            expires_at=now + timedelta(seconds=1),
        )
        result = referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_expires_at_equal_to_created_at_raises(self, referral_service):
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        ref = make_referral(created_at=now, expires_at=now)
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="expires_at"):
            referral_service.send_referral(ref)

    def test_expires_at_before_created_at_raises(self, referral_service):
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        ref = make_referral(
            created_at=now,
            expires_at=now - timedelta(seconds=1),
        )
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="expires_at"):
            referral_service.send_referral(ref)

    def test_expires_at_one_microsecond_after_created_at(self, referral_service):
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        ref = make_referral(
            created_at=now,
            expires_at=now + timedelta(microseconds=1),
        )
        result = referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_expires_at_one_microsecond_before_created_at_raises(self, referral_service):
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        ref = make_referral(
            created_at=now,
            expires_at=now - timedelta(microseconds=1),
        )
        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            referral_service.send_referral(ref)

    def test_referral_with_far_future_expiry_accepted(self, referral_service):
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        ref = make_referral(
            created_at=now,
            expires_at=now + timedelta(days=3650),  # ~10 years
        )
        result = referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_referral_created_at_epoch_start(self, referral_service):
        epoch = datetime(1970, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        ref = make_referral(
            created_at=epoch,
            expires_at=epoch + timedelta(days=1),
        )
        result = referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_referral_created_at_far_future(self, referral_service):
        future = datetime(2099, 12, 31, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        ref = make_referral(
            created_at=future,
            expires_at=future + timedelta(hours=1),
        )
        result = referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_created_at_and_expires_at_differ_by_exactly_one_day(self, referral_service):
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        ref = make_referral(
            created_at=now,
            expires_at=now + timedelta(days=1),
        )
        result = referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_message_sent_at_is_not_before_referral_created_at(
        self, referral_service, mock_message_gateway
    ):
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        ref = make_referral(created_at=now, expires_at=now + timedelta(days=1))
        referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        msg: SecureMessage = mock_message_gateway.send.return_value
        assert msg.sent_at >= ref.created_at

    def test_consent_checked_at_is_not_before_referral_created_at(
        self, referral_service, mock_consent_service
    ):
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        ref = make_referral(created_at=now, expires_at=now + timedelta(days=1))
        referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        rec: ConsentRecord = mock_consent_service.check.return_value
        assert rec.checked_at >= ref.created_at


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 4 – Facility identity constraints
# ===========================================================================

class TestFacilityConstraints:

    def test_same_facility_raises(self, referral_service):
        ref = make_referral(
            referring_facility_id="facility-A",
            receiving_facility_id="facility-A",
        )
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="facilit"):
            referral_service.send_referral(ref)

    def test_different_facilities_accepted(self, referral_service):
        ref = make_referral(
            referring_facility_id="facility-A",
            receiving_facility_id="facility-B",
        )
        result = referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_message_sent_to_receiving_facility(
        self, referral_service, mock_message_gateway
    ):
        ref = make_referral(
            referring_facility_id="facility-A",
            receiving_facility_id="facility-Z",
        )
        referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        sent_msg: SecureMessage = mock_message_gateway.send.return_value
        assert sent_msg.recipient_facility_id == "facility-Z"


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 5 – Consent failure scenarios
# ===========================================================================

class TestConsentFailure:

    def test_no_consent_prevents_message_dispatch(
        self, mock_message_gateway
    ):
        """When consent check raises, the message gateway must not be called."""
        consent_service = MagicMock(spec=ConsentService)
        consent_service.check.side_effect = PermissionError("Patient consent denied.")
        svc = ReferralService(mock_message_gateway, consent_service)
        ref = make_referral()

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            svc.send_referral(ref)

        mock_message_gateway.send.assert_not_called()

    def test_consent_denial_propagates_exception(
        self, mock_message_gateway
    ):
        consent_service = MagicMock(spec=ConsentService)
        consent_service.check.side_effect = PermissionError("Consent denied")
        svc = ReferralService(mock_message_gateway, consent_service)

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError, match="Consent denied"):
            svc.send_referral(make_referral())

    def test_consent_checked_with_correct_patient(
        self, referral_service, mock_consent_service
    ):
        ref = make_referral(patient_id="patient-XYZ")
        referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        args, _ = mock_consent_service.check.call_args
        assert args[0].patient_id == "patient-XYZ"


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 6 – Message gateway failure scenarios
# ===========================================================================

class TestMessageGatewayFailure:

    def test_gateway_failure_raises_and_is_not_swallowed(
        self, mock_consent_service
    ):
        gw = MagicMock(spec=MessageGateway)
        gw.send.side_effect = ConnectionError("Gateway unreachable")
        svc = ReferralService(gw, mock_consent_service)

        with pytest.raises(ConnectionError, match="Gateway unreachable"):
            svc.send_referral(make_referral())

    def test_gateway_called_after_consent_on_success(
        self, referral_service, mock_message_gateway, mock_consent_service
    ):
        ref = make_referral()
        referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        assert mock_consent_service.check.called
        assert mock_message_gateway.send.called


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 7 – Idempotency and uniqueness
# ===========================================================================

class TestReferralUniqueness:

    def test_two_referrals_have_distinct_ids(self):
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        ref1 = make_referral()
        ref2 = make_referral()
        assert ref1.referral_id != ref2.referral_id

    def test_each_send_triggers_independent_consent_check(
        self, referral_service, mock_consent_service
    ):
        ref1 = make_referral()
        ref2 = make_referral()
        referral_service.send_referral(ref1)
        referral_service.send_referral(ref2)
        assert mock_consent_service.check.call_count == 2

    def test_each_send_triggers_independent_message(
        self, referral_service, mock_message_gateway
    ):
        referral_service.send_referral(make_referral())
        referral_service.send_referral(make_referral())
        assert mock_message_gateway.send.call_count == 2


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 8 – Notes / string field edge cases
# ===========================================================================

class TestNotesField:

    def test_empty_notes_accepted(self, referral_service):
        ref = make_referral(notes="")
        result = referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_very_long_notes_accepted(self, referral_service):
        ref = make_referral(notes="x" * 10_000)
        result = referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_notes_with_special_characters_accepted(self, referral_service):
        ref = make_referral(notes="<script>alert('xss')</script> & \"quotes\"")
        result = referral_service.send_referral(ref)
        assert result["success"] is True
```

---

## Design rationale

| Area | What is tested | Why |
|---|---|---|
| **Core requirement** | `send_referral` triggers both the secure message **and** the consent check, exactly once each, and consent runs first | Direct traceability to the stated requirement |
| **Priority (numeric)** | Valid range `[1,5]`, boundary values `1` & `5`, off-by-one `0` & `6`, negative, zero, large positive | Covers all numeric boundary cases per standard boundary-value analysis |
| **Timestamps** | `expires_at == created_at` (equal, invalid), `±1 µs`, `±1 s`, `±1 day`, epoch, far-future, reverse order | Covers all meaningful timestamp boundaries including microsecond precision |
| **Facilities** | Same facility → `ValueError`; different facilities → success; message routed to correct recipient | Identity/uniqueness constraint |
| **Consent failure** | Gateway is **not** called when consent raises; exception propagates | Security invariant — no message without consent |
| **Gateway failure** | Exception propagates and is not swallowed | Reliability / error-transparency |
| **Idempotency** | Two distinct referrals each trigger independent checks | No cross-contamination between referrals |
| **Notes field** | Empty, 10 k characters, special characters | String boundary / injection hardening |

"""
pytest test suite for:
  Requirement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility;
               the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check.
  Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
"""

import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (production code placeholders – replace with real imports)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class Referral:
    referral_id: str
    clinician_id: str
    patient_id: str
    referring_facility_id: str
    receiving_facility_id: str
    priority: int                        # 1 (routine) – 5 (emergency)
    created_at: datetime
    expires_at: datetime
    notes: str = ""
    consent_obtained: bool = False


@dataclass
class SecureMessage:
    message_id: str
    referral_id: str
    sender_id: str
    recipient_facility_id: str
    sent_at: datetime
    encrypted: bool = True
    payload: dict = field(default_factory=dict)


@dataclass
class ConsentRecord:
    consent_id: str
    patient_id: str
    referral_id: str
    consented: bool
    checked_at: datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service interfaces (production code placeholders)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ReferralService:
    def __init__(self, message_gateway, consent_service):
        self._msg = message_gateway
        self._consent = consent_service

    def send_referral(self, referral: Referral) -> dict:
        """
        Validates and sends a referral.
        Returns a result dict with keys: success, message_id, consent_id.
        Raises ValueError for invalid input.
        """
        # --- validation (minimal stub; real impl lives in production code) ---
        if referral.referring_facility_id == referral.receiving_facility_id:
            raise ValueError("Referring and receiving facilities must differ.")
        if not (1 <= referral.priority <= 5):
            raise ValueError(f"Priority {referral.priority} out of range [1,5].")
        if referral.expires_at <= referral.created_at:
            raise ValueError("expires_at must be after created_at.")

        # --- consent check ---
        consent = self._consent.check(referral)

        # --- secure message ---
        message = self._msg.send(referral)

        return {
            "success": True,
            "message_id": message.message_id,
            "consent_id": consent.consent_id,
        }


class MessageGateway:
    def send(self, referral: Referral) -> SecureMessage:
        raise NotImplementedError

class ConsentService:
    def check(self, referral: Referral) -> ConsentRecord:
        raise NotImplementedError


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def mock_message_gateway():
    gw = MagicMock(spec=MessageGateway)
    gw.send.return_value = SecureMessage(
        message_id="msg-001",
        referral_id="ref-001",
        sender_id="clinician-1",
        recipient_facility_id="facility-B",
        sent_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
        encrypted=True,
    )
    return gw


@pytest.fixture
def mock_consent_service():
    cs = MagicMock(spec=ConsentService)
    cs.check.return_value = ConsentRecord(
        consent_id="consent-001",
        patient_id="patient-1",
        referral_id="ref-001",
        consented=True,
        checked_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
    )
    return cs


@pytest.fixture
def referral_service(mock_message_gateway, mock_consent_service):
    return ReferralService(mock_message_gateway, mock_consent_ser
